lines: Start with a breath that goes all the way down. [short pause] Not into your chest... [short pause] [short pause] into your belly. [short pause] Let your stomach expand as the air fills. [short pause] In for five... [short pause] [short pause] and out for five.

Again. [short pause] In... [short pause] [short pause] two... [short pause] [short pause] three... [short pause] [short pause] four... [short pause] [short pause] five. [short pause] Out... [short pause] [short pause] two... [short pause] [short pause] three... [short pause] [short pause] four... [short pause] [short pause] five.
Once more. [short pause] Fill your belly... [short pause] [short pause] and release. [short pause] Feel your body settle with the exhale.
And one last time. [short pause] In... [short pause] [short pause] and out.

Good. [short pause] Just be here for a moment. [short pause] You don't have to be ready yet. [short pause] You don't have to be anything yet. [short pause] Let your body be heavy. [short pause] Let the surface beneath you hold your weight. [short pause] You are allowed to be tired right now.

Maybe you are tired this morning. [short pause] Maybe yesterday took more than it gave. [short pause] Maybe you went to bed with something unresolved, and it followed you into today. [short pause] Maybe you're running on fumes and wondering how you're going to get through another one. [short pause] Maybe the weariness isn't even from yesterday... [short pause] [short pause] it's been building for weeks. [short pause] Months.
If that's you... [short pause] [short pause] this passage was written for you. [short pause] Specifically. [short pause] Intentionally. [short pause] For the weary.
The prophet Isaiah, speaking to a people who had been worn down by exile, by decades of waiting, by the creeping suspicion that God had forgotten them, wrote this:

"He gives strength to the weary and increases the power of the weak. [short pause] Even youths grow tired and weary, and young men stumble and fall... [short pause] [short pause] but those who hope in the Lord will renew their strength. [short pause] They will soar on wings like eagles; they will run and not grow weary; they will walk and not be faint."

Listen to who this promise is for. [short pause] Not the already-strong. [short pause] Not the ones who have it all together. [short pause] Not the ones who bounced out of bed this morning full of energy. [short pause] The weary. [short pause] The weak. [short pause] The ones who stumble. [short pause] The ones who fall.
That might be you today. [short pause] And if it is... [short pause] [short pause] you're exactly who God is speaking to.

Strength is not something you manufacture. [short pause] It's something you receive. [short pause] And the source... [short pause] [short pause] has no limit. [short pause] He doesn't get tired. [short pause] He doesn't grow weary. [short pause] His reserves don't run low. [short pause] And he gives... [short pause] [short pause] not sells, not lends, not trades... [short pause] [short pause] gives... [short pause] [short pause] strength to the weary.

Close your eyes if they aren't already. [short pause] Imagine warmth starting at the center of your chest. [short pause] Not heat... [short pause] [short pause] warmth. [short pause] Like an ember catching light after a long, cold night. [short pause] With each breath, it grows a little brighter.

It spreads to your shoulders. [short pause] Feel the tension there start to soften... [short pause] [short pause] not because you're forcing it, but because something stronger than your stress is moving through you. [short pause] The warmth flows down your arms. [short pause] Into your hands. [short pause] Feel them ready. [short pause] Open and close your fists once... [short pause] [short pause] feel the life in them. [short pause] The grip. [short pause] The capability.

That warmth moves down through your core. [short pause] Your hips. [short pause] Your legs. [short pause] Down into your feet. [short pause] Press them into the ground... [short pause] [short pause] solid, stable, planted. [short pause] Feel the surface beneath you pushing back. [short pause] You are held.

Now imagine standing tall. [short pause] Feel your spine straighten... [short pause] [short pause] one vertebra at a time. [short pause] Your shoulders draw back. [short pause] Your chin lifts slightly. [short pause] Not with tension... [short pause] [short pause] with quiet readiness. [short pause] Like someone who knows they don't have to carry this alone. [short pause] Like someone who just remembered where their strength actually comes from.

Lord... [short pause] [short pause] I'm tired. [short pause] You know how tired. [short pause] I don't have what today requires, and I'm done pretending I do. [short pause] I've been running on my own strength, and it's not enough. [short pause] It was never supposed to be. [short pause] So I'm asking you plainly... [short pause] [short pause] renew me. [short pause] Not with adrenaline. [short pause] Not with another burst of willpower that fades by noon. [short pause] With your strength. [short pause] The kind that carries the weary. [short pause] The kind that lifts the ones who've stopped being able to lift themselves. [short pause] I hope in you today. [short pause] Not because I feel strong... [short pause] [short pause] but because you are. [short pause] Amen.
Three deep breaths now. [short pause] Each one is an act of receiving. [short pause] Not performing. [short pause] Receiving.

Breathe in... [short pause] [short pause] strength for the weary... [short pause] [short pause] hold it at the top for a moment... [short pause] [short pause] and release what's been weighing you down.
Breathe in... [short pause] [short pause] power for the weak... [short pause] [short pause] hold... [short pause] [short pause] and let go of the pressure to do this on your own.
One more. [short pause] Breathe in... [short pause] [short pause] renewal... [short pause] [short pause] hold... [short pause] [short pause] and exhale completely.

Feel your feet on the ground again. [short pause] Feel the steadiness that wasn't there five minutes ago. [short pause] You're not dragging yourself through today. [short pause] You are being carried by something larger than your own reserves. [short pause] Something that doesn't run out.
"Those who hope in the Lord will renew their strength. [short pause] They will soar on wings like eagles; they will run and not grow weary; they will walk and not be faint."

May that promise carry you today... [short pause] [short pause] through every meeting, every conversation, every moment your energy dips and the weariness creeps back in. [short pause] The Lord who gives strength to the weary... [short pause] [short pause] is with you now. [short pause] He was with you before you asked. [short pause] And he will be with you long after this meditation ends.
Go in his renewal. [short pause] And when you grow tired again... [short pause] [short pause] come back to this. [short pause] He gives strength to the weary. [short pause] Every time.
